ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Continued To Nov-07-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Aug-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROGER HARTMAN'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Roger Hartman's motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction is granted. In a check-box complaint, plaintiff alleges he was injured at an Arizona property rented from Colorado-resident defendant over Airbnb, which is headquartered in San Francisco. The parties dispute whether plaintiff was resident at the time in Arizona or California. Defendant appears to concede that a contract can be formed over Airbnb. Defendant says he has been in California twice in his life, for a Disneyland trip and first aid training - both many years ago. "When a defendant moves to quash on jurisdictional grounds, the plaintiff has the initial burden of demonstrating facts justifying the exercise of jurisdiction." (Vons Companies, Inc. v. SeabestFoods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 449.) Asserting minimum contacts for specific jurisdiction, plaintiff complains that defendant "is purposefully silent on the number of times he has rented to California residents." (Opp. 4:8-9, 5:15-17.) But it was plaintiff's burden to adduce facts justifying exercise of jurisdiction (Vons, 14 Cal.4th at 449) - a burden not carried. Moreover, the only Airbnb case cited by either side found no personal jurisdiction over homeowners. (Zamora v. Lewis (2019) 146 NE.3d 231.) Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)

The Order to Show Cause is re-set to June 6, 2023 at 10:30am in Department 610.
